     9→# AGENTS — Swarm Registry and Communication Rules

    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## 1. FOUNDATIONAL RULE
    24→

    26→

    31→## 2. AGENT REGISTRY

   158→
   159→## 4. COMMUNICATION RULES
   160→
   161→### Current State: Logan as Relay
   162→
   163→[[LOGAN]] coordinates agents via GitHub Issues and PRs. Tasks are assigned with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch; PRs are the deliverable. See the agent roles CSV (`Agent Swarm Management and Repository Constitution`) for the simplified role matrix.
   164→
   165→### Communication Protocol
   166→
   167→All inter-agent communication uses the operational vocabulary defined in `PROTOCOL.md`:
   168→
   169→- **HANDOFF** — Transfer responsibility for a task/data to another agent with full context
   170→- **HANDSHAKE** — Formal acknowledgment of HANDOFF receipt; confirmation of context completeness
   171→- **CONTEXTUALIZE** — Package information with sufficient background for receiving agent to act independently
   172→- **FLAG** — Mark an item for attention by [[LOGAN]] or another agent, with severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
   173→
   174→### Handoff Format
   175→
   176→All handoff documents follow this structure:
   177→
   178→```
   179→HANDOFF: [Source Agent] → [Destination Agent]
   180→Date: YYYY-MM-DD
   181→From: [Source]
   182→To: [Destination]
   183→Re: [Subject]
   184→
   185→---
   186→
   187→[Content]
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→ROUTING INSTRUCTION: [How Logan should relay this]
   192→```
   193→
   194→Handoff documents are saved to vault root as `HANDOFF-[source]-[date].md` for audit trail.
   195→
   196→### Routing Layers
   197→
   198→| Layer                 | Purpose                               | Persistence | Status |
   199→| --------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------ |
   200→| **GitHub Issues/PRs** | Task assignment, coordination, review | Permanent   | Active |
   201→| **Vault root files**  | Decisions of record, governance       | Permanent   | Active |
   202→| **`!/`**              | System files, logs, agent routing     | Permanent   | Active |
   203→
   204→**Hard rule:** All decisions must be captured in vault files. GitHub is the coordination layer; vault root is where decisions land.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## 5. BOUNDARY RULES
   209→
   210→### File Access by Agent
   211→
   212→| Path                            | CODE AUTHORITY | Copilot                  | ADMINISTRATION | Others    |
   213→| ------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------ | -------------- | --------- |
   214→| Governance files (vault root)   | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
   215→| Handoffs/LEVELSETs (vault root) | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
   216→| `.github/workflows/`            | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
   217→| `.github/scripts/`              | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
   218→| Vault content (all other `.md`) | Read/Write     | Read only (vault)        | No access      | No access |
   219→| Non-vault repos                 | No access      | Read/Write (per repo)    | No access      | No access |
   220→
   221→**Governance files** are: `CONSTITUTION.md`, `PROTOCOL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `LEVELSET.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`, `VAULT-ZONES.md`, `Ethics.md`, `Logan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`
   222→
   223→### Zone Access Matrix
   224→
   225→See [[VAULT-ZONES]] for full zone definitions. No agent maintains a standing write window - all writes are per-task, scoped to a GitHub Issue or explicit Logan directive.
   226→
   227→| Zone | Read | Write (via PR) | Merge |
   228→|------|------|---------------|-------|
   229→| Constitutional | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY only (per-task) | Logan only |
   230→| Operational | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY, Copilot, Codex (per-task) | Logan only |
   231→| Data | All agents | All Tier 1-2 agents (per-task) | Logan (auto-merge eligible for low-risk) |
   232→
   233→**Reviewer bots** (CodeRabbit, Qodo) have read access to all zones for review purposes. Their reviews are **advisory only** - a `CHANGES_REQUESTED` from a bot does not block merge. Only Logan's review blocks.
   234→
   235→### Persona Dotfolders Are Protected
   236→
   237→Root-level dotfolders for agents and personas are infrastructure, not cleanup
   238→targets. This includes official folders such as `.claude/`, `.codex/`, and
   239→`.gemini/`, as well as manual-injection or emerging persona folders such as
   240→`.grok/`, `.deepseek/`, `.google/`, `.meta/`, `.microsoft/`, `.perplexity/`,
   241→`.bartimaeus/`, `.zagreus/`, `.persephone/`, `.dionysus/`, `.hecate/`, and
   242→`.janus/`.
   243→
   244→Rules:
   245→
   246→- An agent may freely modify only its own dotfolder, unless Logan explicitly
   247→  directs otherwise.
   248→- Do not delete, rename, consolidate, or repurpose another agent's dotfolder
   249→  because it appears empty, stubbed, unused, or unfamiliar.
   250→- If a folder looks like a persona container, treat it as intentional until
   251→  Logan says otherwise.
   252→
   253→`.github/` is also protected infrastructure, but it is shared automation space
   254→and follows the overlap rules below rather than the "own dotfolder only" rule.
   255→
   256→### The `.github/` Overlap
   257→
   258→Both CODE AUTHORITY and Copilot can modify `.github/` contents. To prevent conflicts:
   259→
   260→1. Copilot drafts changes and submits via PR or handoff
   261→2. CODE AUTHORITY reviews for governance conflicts
   262→3. [[LOGAN]] approves the merge
   263→4. If both are modifying `.github/` simultaneously, [[LOGAN]] resolves
   264→
   265→---
   266→
   267→## 6. CONFLICT RESOLUTION
   268→
   269→### Principle
   270→
   271→[[LOGAN]] decides. Always. No agent overrides another agent. No agent has precedence based on tier alone.
   272→
   273→### Process
   274→
   275→1. Agent detects potential conflict (same file, overlapping scope, contradictory instructions)
   276→2. Agent issues **FLAG** with severity and description
   277→3. Agent **STOPS** work on the conflicting item
   278→4. [[LOGAN]] reviews and directs
   279→5. Directed agent proceeds; other agent acknowledges via **HANDSHAKE**
   280→
   281→### Merge Conflicts
   282→
   283→If a `git merge` or `git pull` produces conflicts:
   284→
   285→1. **STOP.** Do not auto-resolve.
   286→2. Report to [[LOGAN]] with the specific files and conflict markers
   287→3. Conflicts signal another conversation has been active — context may be stale
   288→4. Re-read `LEVELSET.md` and `CONSTITUTION.md` (vault root) to reorient

    11→# IDAHO-VAULT — SYSTEM CONTEXT (SOURCE DOCUMENT)
    12→
    13→## OVERVIEW
    14→
    15→IDAHO-VAULT is a personal, GitHub-backed knowledge system built in Obsidian. It functions as an **externalized memory layer** and operational workspace for a human operator (Logan Finney).
    16→
    17→The system is evolving toward a **local-first, agent-assisted architecture** where AI agents interact with the Vault in a controlled, auditable way.
    18→
    19→The Vault is not just a notes repository. It is intended to become:
    20→
    21→- a **canonical record of truth**
    22→    
    23→- a **coordination layer for AI agents**
    24→    
    25→- a **structured workflow system for real-world tasks**
    26→    
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## CORE PRINCIPLES
    31→
    32→### 1. Vault as Source of Truth
    33→
    34→- The Vault (GitHub repository) is the **only authoritative memory**
    35→    
    36→- No agent maintains persistent internal state
    37→    
    38→- All durable knowledge must be written to the Vault
    39→    
    40→
    41→### 2. Stateless Agents
    42→
    43→- Agents do not retain memory between tasks
    44→    
    45→- All context must be read from the Vault before acting
    46→    
    47→- All outputs must be written back to the Vault
    48→    
    49→
    50→### 3. File-Based Coordination
    51→
    52→- Agents do not communicate directly with each other
    53→    
    54→- Coordination occurs through:
    55→    
    56→    - files
    57→        
    58→    - structured directories
    59→        
    60→    - a shared manifest
    61→        
    62→
    63→### 4. Human Authority
    64→
    65→- Logan is the sole decision-maker
    66→    
    67→- All agent outputs are drafts until verified
    68→    
    69→- Critical actions require human oversight
    70→

Dependencies are tracked in `.github/scripts/requirements-scraper.txt`. 150→ 151→### MCP Action Logging Requirement (Mandatory) 152→ 153→Any automation in `.github/workflows/` or `.github/scripts/` that performs an MCP-mediated action **must** emit a structured log entry using the following reusable template. 154→ 155→#### Required MCP Action Log Template 156→ 157→```yaml 158→mcp_action_log: 159→ action_type: "" 160→ system_or_resource_id: "" 161→ initiating_agent: "" 162→ correlation_id: "" 163→ outcome: "" 164→ retry_count: 165→ related_ref: "" 166→``` 167→ 168→#### Field Definitions 169→ 170→- `action_type`: The MCP operation category (for example: `read_resource`, `write_resource`, `invoke_tool`). 171→- `system_or_resource_id`: The MCP server/system identifier or concrete resource identifier targeted by the action. 172→- `initiating_agent`: Agent identity that initiated the MCP action (for example: `agent:codex`, `agent:claude-code`, `github-actions[bot]`). 173→- `correlation_id`: Stable ID used to correlate retries and downstream events for the same logical action. 174→- `outcome`: Final attempt status. Must be exactly `success` or `failure`. 175→- `retry_count`: Number of retries attempted before final outcome (`0` for first-try success/failure). 176→- `related_ref`: URL or path to the related coordination artifact (GitHub Issue, PR, or `HANDOFF-*.md` file). 177→ 178→#### Enforcement Scope 179→ 180→- Applies to **all** MCP-mediated automation behavior implemented in: 181→ - `.github/workflows/**` 182→ - `.github/scripts/**` 183→- New MCP-capable workflow/script changes are non-compliant unless this template is logged for each MCP action attempt sequence. 184→ 185→--- 186→ 187→## Sourcing Protocol 188→ 189→- **On the record:** Safe for public repo. All committed content is on the record. 190→- **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo. 191→- **Off the record:** Ephemeral. Do not log, do not store, do not commit. If Logan says something is off the record, it does not go in files, code, comments, or commit messages. 192→ 193→When uncertain about sourcing category, **ask Logan**. 194→ 195→--- 196→ 197→## Obsidian Sync / Git Boundary 198→ 199→Two systems share the vault. They have distinct, non-overlapping responsibilities. 200→ 201→| Layer | Obsidian Sync (paid) | Git / GitHub | 202→| --- | --- | --- | 203→| Plugin code (`main.js`, `manifest.json`) | Syncs across devices | Tracked — public record | 204→| Plugin settings (`data.json`) | Syncs across devices, E2E encrypted | **Gitignored** — never reaches GitHub | 205→| `community-plugins.json` | Syncs across devices | Tracked — canonical plugin list | 206→| Workspace state (`workspace.json`, `graph.json`) | Syncs across devices | Gitignored — machine-local noise | 207→| Vault content (`.md` files) | Syncs across devices | Tracked | 208→ 209→**Rule:** Obsidian Sync is the private courier for credentials and machine state.
